# Trenith Tools — factual reference

Trenith Tools is a free global utility platform operated by Trenith Technologies Private Limited, CIN U62099TS2026PTC216554, registered in India.

The global hostname is https://tools.trenith.com and the India-focused hostname is https://tools.trenith.in. Each deployment should use a self-referencing canonical and reciprocal en / en-IN hreflang links.

The platform contains three kinds of workflow. Device tools use browser APIs or locally loaded WebAssembly and do not intentionally send selected source files to Trenith. Public Source tools send a user-entered public URL to a Trenith route for the requested scan or retrieval. BYOK tools send user-initiated requests to the provider the user connects; provider terms and billing apply.

Trenith Watch Together is a separate free real-time feature for authorized OTT web playback. Every participant opens the title through their own provider account. A Manifest V3 browser companion synchronizes HTML5 play, pause, seek and playback rate; it does not screen-capture, download, decrypt or retransmit the title. Launch adapters cover YouTube, Netflix, Prime Video and JioHotstar, while other listed providers use an honestly labeled generic HTML5 beta. Native streaming mobile and TV apps are not controlled. Room chat, reactions, playback state and WebRTC negotiation are AES-GCM encrypted in the browser and expire from signaling storage; temporary display names, roles, token hashes and connection timestamps remain operational metadata. Optional camera/microphone media uses WebRTC and is not recorded by Trenith. Rooms expire after six hours, support up to 25 synchronized participants and limit live media to six.

The metadata remover accepts files and folders, inventories recognized metadata, removes supported fields, rescans the output and offers individual or ZIP downloads. Cleaning is routed per format: the embedded ExifTool engine rewrites images, camera formats and MP4-family media; a dedicated document rewriter strips PDF information dictionaries and XMP; Word, Excel and PowerPoint document properties are scrubbed directly; and MP3, FLAC, Ogg, Opus, WAV, AIFF and WebM audio is rewritten as a lossless FFmpeg stream copy with metadata and chapters dropped. It does not promise removal of visible content, steganography, proprietary databases or every application-specific trace.

Free on-device SEO tools include a SERP snippet preview with character and approximate pixel checks for titles and descriptions, and a keyword density analyzer reporting word counts, reading time, estimated readability, repeated phrases and the questions a text answers. Both run entirely in the browser.

The AI Studio runs every workflow with whichever provider connection exists and states which provider produces the strongest result. Text-capable keys from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini or OpenRouter run all text workflows and produce structured production plans for media workflows. Vision-capable keys can read text from JPEG, PNG or WebP scans. Text to speech plays through built-in browser voices with no key, while an ElevenLabs key returns downloadable MP3 audio. Compatible custom endpoints can run full media jobs.

The audio joiner accepts files and folders in a user-controlled order. It first uses native browser decoding and automatically retries rejected files with a locally loaded FFmpeg WebAssembly compatibility engine. Smaller compatible jobs are joined in memory. Jobs over the defined file-count or size threshold use a compatible desktop File System Access API, preflight files independently, report unreadable names, optionally skip unreadable files, and stream PCM output directly to a WAV or RF64-compatible destination. There is no artificial file-count quota, but disk space, browser support and codecs remain practical constraints.

The audio converter produces MP3, WAV, FLAC, Ogg Vorbis, Opus and M4A/AAC output on the device. Audio trimming and volume adjustment produce lossless WAV output. The public audio scanner can package discovered authorized files into a ZIP without bypassing source controls.

The public `/status` page explains the engine and dependency for every workspace. `/changelog` and `/feed.xml` publish release history, and `/api/tools` exposes a CORS-readable JSON catalog.

Trenith Tools is free. Revenue can come from Trenith service inquiries, clearly disclosed contextual sponsorship or affiliate relationships, and consented aggregate analytics or campaign measurement. Trenith does not sell personal data or use file contents, private metadata, API keys, prompts or outputs for advertising.

Minimum age is 18. Privacy contacts are privacy@trenith.in, legal@trenith.in and grevience@trenith.in. The public Privacy Notice, Terms, Cookie Policy, Privacy Choices, Security, Subprocessors, Copyright and Accessibility pages are controlling sources for current site practices.
